channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Onto the knockout round: 4 takeaways from the FIFA World Cup so far
item.description: The anxieties before the World Cup were many. But with the knockout round set to begin Sunday, it's fair to say: The North American World Cup has been a thrill.
item.content:encoded:
The anxieties before the World Cup were many. But with the knockout round set to begin Sunday, it's fair to say: The North American World Cup has been a thrill.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: As America turns 250, protesters make their case
item.description: Demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. today say they're offering a different vision for America ahead of its 250th birthday than the Trump administration's Freedom 250 events. NPR's Marissa Lang visited one rally near the White House.
item.content:encoded: Demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. today say they're offering a different vision for America ahead of its 250th birthday than the Trump administration's Freedom 250 events. NPR's Marissa Lang visited one rally near the White House.
item.dc:creator: Marissa Lang

item.title: The strike that reshaped the labor movement
item.description: What can a steelworkers strike from 1892 tell us about today's economy? NPR's Don Gonyea visits Homestead, Pennsylvania where one of America's most important labor fights unfolded.
item.content:encoded: What can a steelworkers strike from 1892 tell us about today's economy? NPR's Don Gonyea visits Homestead, Pennsylvania where one of America's most important labor fights unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Schapitl

item.title: Critical fire weather complicates firefighting efforts in massive Utah wildfire
item.description: Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.
item.content:encoded:
Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.

(Image credit: Ty ONeil)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Texas will require students to read Bible passages in a new state curriculum
item.description: Texas students will be required to read Bible passages and learn about the role of Christianity in the state's history under new reading lists and social studies curriculum expected to be approved Friday.
item.content:encoded: Texas students will be required to read Bible passages and learn about the role of Christianity in the state's history under new reading lists and social studies curriculum expected to be approved Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bill Zeeble

item.title: Native Americans mark the 150th anniversary of the Battle of Little Bighorn
item.description: On the 150th anniversary of the Battle of Little Bighorn, Native American youth gathered at the site of the battle to mark the historic victory and celebrate the resilience of their cultures.
item.content:encoded: On the 150th anniversary of the Battle of Little Bighorn, Native American youth gathered at the site of the battle to mark the historic victory and celebrate the resilience of their cultures.
item.dc:creator: Kadin Mills

item.title: Oklahoma's decades-long lawsuit over pollution in Illinois River Watershed faces hurdles
item.description: A lawsuit over pollution in an Oklahoma watershed by the poultry industry has been dragging on for two decades as the case could set precedent for other states.
item.content:encoded: A lawsuit over pollution in an Oklahoma watershed by the poultry industry has been dragging on for two decades as the case could set precedent for other states.
item.dc:creator: Anna Pope

item.title: As Supreme Court expands Trump's immigration power, experts warn of steeper U.S. population decline
item.description: The U.S. population was already aging and tilting toward decline. After the Supreme Court confirmed Trump's power to deport hundreds of thousands of foreign migrants, population decline could accelerate.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. population was already aging and tilting toward decline. After the Supreme Court confirmed Trump's power to deport hundreds of thousands of foreign migrants, population decline could accelerate.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: How coach Mauricio Pochettino made believers out of the U.S. World Cup team
item.description: Pochettino was the biggest name the U.S. men's soccer team had ever hired. His rebuild was bumpy at times — but now, with the U.S. headed to the World Cup knockout stage, the players are all in.
item.content:encoded:
Pochettino was the biggest name the U.S. men's soccer team had ever hired. His rebuild was bumpy at times — but now, with the U.S. headed to the World Cup knockout stage, the players are all in.

(Image credit: Russell Lewis)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: What would George Washington say? It's a busy year for people who portray him
item.description: America's 250th birthday has brought more events and new crowds for Revolutionary reenactors and interpreters. They say Washington's life holds important lessons for our current political divide.
item.content:encoded:
America's 250th birthday has brought more events and new crowds for Revolutionary reenactors and interpreters. They say Washington's life holds important lessons for our current political divide.

(Image credit: Michelle Gustafson for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden
